# Ellis — AI notes for in-person meetings
## Fox SaaS Solutions
1 subscribers

### Description
16 views
Posted: 8 Jul 2026
AI notes for in-person meetings
Ellis is an AI notetaker for in-person meetings. Record your meeting on iPhone or Apple Watch, get a clean transcript with each speaker identified, then ask anything — what was decided, what you missed, how it went. No laptop, no extra hardware.

This product launched without a demo video, so we made one for free.
Make your own launch video in about 30 seconds — paste your site at 
ellis.coach [...] ### Transcript:
Alice, the note taker for real life meetings. Record the room. Alice hands back a clean transcript with each speaker identified. Your best meetings happen across a table, not over a screen. Alice was built for those. Hit record on your iPhone or Apple Watch. Coffee meetups, sales calls, doctor visits, interviews. No laptop, no extra hardware. Record it, get a clean transcript, then ask anything. What was decided, what you missed, how it went. Walk into your next meeting and just be present. Alice remembers the rest.

results.title: The smartest meeting tool you might not have heard of | Samuel Ellis
results.content: The most dangerous part of any meeting is the 10 minutes after it ends. Momentum is high, but action items are scattered across notes. Good ideas get lost. Dropped balls become inevitable. Here's a simple AI-powered system to turn every meeting transcript into a productive outcome. The Playbook: Meeting Notes → Actions 1. Record your meetings and get an automatic transcript. 2. Paste the full transcript into your AI tool. 3. Add this prompt at the end: "Act as my executive assistant. Review the meeting transcript above and provide the following in a clean, scannable format: 1. A one-paragraph executive summary of the key discussion points and final decisions. 2. A bulleted list of all specific action items, with the name of the person assigned to each one. 3. A list of any key deadlines [...] 🧠 The smartest meeting tool you might not have heard of I’ve been using Granola for the past few months, and it’s quietly improved how I take notes during video calls, so I wanted to share my experience in case you haven't come across it yet. At its heart, it's a meeting notetaker with a lovely interface, but instead of adding a 'bot' to your meetings, Granola sits on your computer (Mac/Windows) or phone, listens, transcribes, and enhances your OWN notes. Here’s what I've found made it different: ✍️ No awkward AI bots — nothing joins your calls, so it’s simple, private, and friction-free. I give a quick “FYI, I’m using an AI notepad for notes” at the start if I feel like I'm likely to want to revisit the conversation. 💬 More control — I jot quick bullets during the meeting, and Granola [...] 46% of employees have watched a bot attend meetings for their colleague. The AI wasn't taking notes. It was the actual attendee. We've reached peak meeting madness. Employees now spend 4.3 hours weekly in meetings. That's almost as much time as they spend with friends. The problem? 77% of meetings just result in scheduling more meetings. So people are sending AI assistants instead. Nearly half have witnessed this firsthand. I get it. When your actual work gets buried under endless calls, something has to give. But here's what's interesting: • 40% find AI meeting attendance rude • Yet adoption keeps growing • The AI meeting assistant market hit $3.16 billion in 2025 • It's projected to reach $72 billion by 2034 Companies like Salesforce report 30% time savings on post-meeting tasks.

results.title: The 7 Best AI Note-Taker for In-Person Meetings (Plus Buying Guide!)
results.content:
Otter.ai is one of the most popular AI meeting assistants.

While it has a lot of great features for remote team meetings, it was built primarily for online meetings rather than in-person meetings. It’s true that Otter can record in-person meetings, but you need to use the mobile app and record via your phone. That brings us back to the problem of poor phone mics, which can result in bad audio quality and cratering AI accuracy.  

Otter works by joining your Zoom, Google Meet, or Microsoft Teams calls as a bot. During meetings, it transcribes and summarizes in real time. Afterwards, everything is searchable, editable, and shareable. Otter also integrates with tons of apps and tools, so you can transcribe and generate notes in many digital spaces. [...] ### #5) Granola

Granola, an ai note taker for meetings

Granola is an AI-powered “notepad” for in-person and online meetings. It is designed to enhance your own note-taking with AI transcription and summaries instead of just producing raw transcripts. If you want to continue taking your own notes, Granola might be for you. If you’re looking for an AI note-taker to do the work for you (so you can engage fully in meetings), then you might want to skip this one. 

Granola captures your computer’s audio in the background while you take notes. It then turns what was said and what you wrote into clear summaries, action items, and decisions. This can help improve the AI’s accuracy because you’re supporting the app with manual note taking. That’s also the kicker: You have to do more work. [...] | Otter | Free: 30-minute limit and 300 transcription minutes/month  Business: $20 per user/month for unlimited recordings and (4 hr limit) | Meeting assistant for transcription and summaries, and works with lots of apps (Zoom, Teams, Google Meet, etc.) | You don’t like bots joining your meetings |
| Granola | Free: limited meetings and history   Business: $14/month for unlimited AI meeting notes | AI improves your own written notes with context and integrations (great if you’re already taking notes) | You want to commit fully to the meeting and avoid taking notes manually |

results.title: Tested 5 AI note taking apps for in-person meetings – here's my verdict
results.content:
Skip to main contentTested 5 AI note taking apps for in-person meetings – here’s my verdict : r/ProductivityApps

Open menu Open navigation. Figured I’d share in case anyone’s looking for something similar.

1.   Bluedot ai note taker. This one’s been the standout. It records in the background, no bot joining and works whether you’re on Zoom, Meet, Teams, or just sitting across the table from someone. Handles both audio and video, supports 70+ languages, and the transcripts are super accurate. The summaries actually feel like summaries, not just reworded transcripts. Bonus: you can upload recordings too. TL;DR: Best all rounder if you want something that works for both in-person and online meetings without any “bot in the room” awkwardness. [...] 2.   Otter ai Still the household name. Great for online team calls and has loads of integrations, but sends a bot to join meetings. Solid accuracy in English, less so for other languages. TL;DR: Great if you’re English-only and mostly remote, maybe overkill for solo/in-person.

3.   Minutes AI Meeting Note Taker Polished design and decent AI chat, but multilingual accuracy isn’t great. TL;DR: Pretty UI, works okay for English-only notes.

4.   Tablo AI Meeting Notes Feels like it could be a native OS app, simple and clean. Good multilingual support and follow up features. TL;DR: Strong pick for multilingual 1-on-1s, but less feature-rich than Bluedot.

5.   Sona Insight Best design of the bunch, but kept failing after recordings. TL;DR: Gorgeous app, but reliability isn’t there yet. [...] You can start using ithere.

results.title: The 7 Best AI Notetakers for In-Person Meetings | Revenue
results.content:
### Best For

Revenue and operations teams that want automated meeting capture tied to CRM workflows, conversation analytics, and searchable meeting archives across both virtual and in-person formats.

Explore more Fireflies.ai competitors & alternatives

## 4. Notion AI

Notion AI brings generative AI capabilities directly into the Notion workspace, allowing teams to draft, summarize, and organize meeting notes without leaving the tool they already use for documentation. While Notion AI is not a standalone transcription tool, it integrates with meeting notes workflows to help teams process and structure information captured during in-person sessions. [...] View more Rilla alternatives

## 7. tl;dv

tl;dv is an AI meeting recorder focused on making meetings easier to consume and share asynchronously. It records and transcribes meetings, generates AI summaries, and allows users to create timestamped clips that can be shared with teammates who were not present.

While tl;dv was designed primarily for video-based meetings, it also supports audio recording for in-person sessions, making it useful for teams that want to create shareable highlights from live conversations, training sessions, or field meetings.

### Key Features [...] ## 2. Otter.ai

Otter.ai is one of the most widely used AI note-taking platforms and a strong choice for in-person meetings. Its mobile app uses the device microphone to capture ambient audio in real time, making it easy to deploy in any meeting room without additional hardware or setup.

Otter transcribes conversations live, identifies individual speakers, and generates summaries with action items automatically. It works particularly well for structured in-person sessions like team standups, client briefings, and sales meetings where participants are grouped around a table.

### Key Features
